text: The video shows a fluorescent lymph node in the sigmoid mesocolon near the origin of the inferior mesenteric artery, highlighted using Neuron-Ferret fluorescence laparoscopy and cybernetic, real-time augmented reality during laparoscopic abdominal perineal resection for rectal cancer.
The surgeon examines the right pelvic sidewall, identifying a fluorescent lymph node along the line of the iliac artery. This node is visually distinct due to its fluorescence.

The surgeon uses a near-infrared camera, the Pinpoint System by Novodac, to visualize fluorescence in the pelvic region after submucosal injection of Indocyanine Green. The camera identifies the tracer through specific illumination and lens filtration, highlighting targeted areas in the patient with very low rectal cancer.

The surgical video displays a real-time augmented reality view, combining standard white light images with near-infrared images to create a false color composite. This visual guide assists the surgeon in identifying anatomical structures during the procedure.

The surgeon performs lymphatic mapping on a second patient with very low rectal cancer, highlighting lymphatic pathways to the midline and pelvic sidewalls using fluorescence laparoscopy. The patient previously underwent a long course of chemotherapy.
The surgeon performs selective lymphadenectomy to remove a specific fluorescent lymph node outside the standard total mesorectal and mesocolic plane of dissection, using augmented reality guidance.
The video shows the specific afferent lymphatic channel leading directly from beneath the rectal cancer site, where the fluorophore was injected, to the iliac chain lymph node.

The surgeon uses a near-infrared imaging system to guide the dissection of the inferior mesenteric artery during the abdominal perineal rectal resection. Lymph nodes along the artery are clearly visible, and the imaging assists in identifying the correct embryological plane between the parietal and visceral fascia. The technique ensures inclusion of the apical lymph node at the artery's origin within the resection specimen by guiding the arterial transection level.
